title: Top 10 Solo RPG Things (games, tools, systems etc.)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=v9INdKeBJEI

Daniel (@dungeondive) explores the thriving ecosystem of solo role-playing tools and games that have transformed the hobby. The first half highlights essential generators: the Sandbox Generator excels at hex crawl and dungeon creation, while Untold Encounters and the Deck of Many Dungeons provide quick, snappy encounter tables for towns, wilderness, and dungeons. Daniel loves how Inkwell's side quest and NPC decks deliver immediate usability—no flipping through scattered rules. The outdoor encounter cards from Phil Read function as both inspiration and physical map elements, while Table Fables One and Two remain consistently useful reference books for rolling up adventures on the fly.

Moving to actual games, Fallen offers a lightweight point-crawl system with intuitive skill-based exploration, while Space Aces delivers pulpy sci-fi episodic adventure with a rising "heat" wanted level. Scarlet Heroes stands out as the game that finally clicked for Daniel—a sword-and-sorcery system designed specifically for soloists who want to feel powerful like Conan. Finally, Mork Borg with Solitary Defilement dominates the top spot, a beautifully simple system where Daniel adds a custom "determination" stat that forces him to take narrative control and turn defeats into story hooks, creating genuinely memorable adventures.

----

Which solo RPG tool or game has transformed how you approach dungeon exploration, and are there underrated generators or systems more people should discover?
